COURT'S STATEMENT OF INTENDED DECISION (REVISED JANUARY 11, 2005) FILED (MELLON-514)

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: DECLARATORY JUDGMENT: IT IS ORDERED THAT DEFENDANT FINANCIAL PACIFIC HAS NO OBLIGATION UNDER THE LAW NOR UNDER THE TERMS OF ITS CONTRACT WITH THE INSURED TO PAY 100% OF ALL COSTS (INCLUDING ALL ATTORNEYS FEES CHARGED IN DOWNEY BRAND'S BILLINGS) REASONABLY PAID OR INCURRED BY TIG IN DEFENSE OF THE INSURED; AND IT IS FURTHER ORDERED THAT DEFENDANT FINANCIAL PACIFIC HAS NO OBLIGATION BY REASON OF OR ON ACCOUNT OF ANY CONTRACT THAT FINANCIAL PACIFIC HAS MADE WITH PLAINTIFF TIG EITHER 1) TO ENGAGE STEINHEIMER [DOWNEY BRAND] TO DEFEND THE BAKER ACTION OR 2) TO PAY 80% OR ANY PART AT ALL OF HIS [ITS] BILLINGS FOR SERVICES RENDERED IN THAT DEFENSE. DEFENDANT FINANCIAL PACIFIC IS THE PREVAILING PARTY IN THIS ACTION AND IS THEREFORE ENTITLED TO RECOVER ITS COSTS OF SUIT IN SUCH AMOUNT AS MAY SUBSEQUENTLY BE FIXED. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT.
MEMORANDUM OF COSTS AND DISBURSEMENTS, $7,076.52 TOTAL COSTS, MATURE DATE MAY-10-2005, FILED BY DEFENDANT FINANCIAL PACIFIC INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION

MASTER CALENDAR MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-16-2004 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFF TIG INSURANCE'S MOTION FOR TRIAL PREFERENCE AND TRIAL SETTING IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 1062.3(A). DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: DONALD L. MABRY, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF TIG INSURANCE COMPANY. BRYCE D. CARROLL, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT FINANCIAL PACIFIC INSURANCE COMPANY. MOTION ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS AND COMPLETION OF THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT FINANCIAL PACIFIC INSURANCE COMPANY EMPLOYEE GENE EBERLE AND DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER ARE EACH GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE WITNESS IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR THE RESUMPTION OF HIS DEPOSITION. THAT PART OF THE MOTION IS NOT OPPOSED. THE COURT FINDS THAT QUESTIONS WHICH GO TO THE HANDLING OF THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION ARE IRRELEVANT AND BEYOND THE SCOPE OF THE DEPOSITION. HOWEVER, QUESTIONS WHICH GO TO THE LEVEL OF HIS AUTHORITY ARE PROPER. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION ON OCTOBER 5, 2004. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
